description: Overall structure: Long torpedo shaped body with small head & long rear end extension, and long narrow angular wings, pointed at tips. Shape suggested large Arctic Tern.  Head was proportionately small and was whitish. Did not see bill well but it looked dark in contrast to whitish head.  Upper mantle was steely gray with contrasting dark primaries and solid dark trailing edge to wings. At certain angles a white line was visible on outer primary but this line was not always visible. The line was probably one but not more than two primary shaft(s).  Underparts were whitish with obscure diffuse light gray breast band. I did not see undertail coverts well. Underwings were heavily barred to primaries which were dark but centered with a prominent white flash.  The tail was narrow with a projection of central tail feathers of between 1-2 inches. The tips of these central tail feathers appeared blunt.Behaviors: Flight was erratic and buoyant with bird frequently changing directions amid mostly flapping but occasionally soaring flight pattern. It landed on water twice during observation, but was down for no more than a few seconds before taking flight again.Call: None heardPlumage: light morph juvenile
